questions.question: Which layer of the OSI model is responsible for logical addressing and routing?
questions.explanation: The Network Layer (Layer 3) handles logical addressing (IP addresses) and routing decisions. Routers operate at this layer.

questions.question: What is the PDU (Protocol Data Unit) at the Transport Layer?
questions.explanation: At the Transport Layer, data is called a segment. TCP segments include sequence numbers for reliable delivery.

questions.question: Which protocol operates at Layer 7 (Application Layer)?
questions.explanation: HTTP (Hypertext Transfer Protocol) operates at the Application Layer, providing web communication services.

questions.question: ARP operates at which layer?
questions.explanation: ARP operates at the Data Link Layer (Layer 2), translating IP addresses to MAC addresses.

questions.question: Which layer is responsible for establishing, managing, and terminating sessions?
questions.explanation: The Session Layer (Layer 5) manages dialog control between applications, establishing, maintaining, and terminating connections.
